questions.question: What pattern does Spring MVC implement for web apps?
questions.explanation: DispatcherServlet routes requests to @Controller handlers, binds data to Model, and resolves Views (or REST body).

questions.question: Which annotation marks a class as a web controller in Spring MVC?
questions.explanation: @Controller returns view names; @RestController = @Controller + @ResponseBody for JSON/XML APIs.

questions.question: What does @RequestMapping on a class do?
options: Prefixes URL paths for all handler methods in the class
questions.explanation: Class-level @RequestMapping("/api/orders") combines with method-level mappings to form full paths.

questions.question: Difference between @GetMapping and @RequestMapping(method=GET)?
options: No difference in behavior for GET
@GetMapping is composed shortcut; same HTTP GET mapping
questions.explanation: @GetMapping is a specialized @RequestMapping with method GET—cleaner and preferred in modern Spring.

questions.question: What will this endpoint return path variable id for GET /products/7?
questions.explanation: @PathVariable binds URI template variable. Type conversion can parse "7" to Long if parameter is Long id.

questions.question: How is query parameter page bound?
questions.explanation: @RequestParam("page") int page reads ?page=2. defaultValue and required=false are optional attributes.

questions.question: What is the Model in Spring MVC used for?
options: Passing attributes from controller to view (server-side rendering)
questions.explanation: Model (or ModelAndView) holds request-scoped attributes for Thymeleaf/JSP views. REST APIs often return DTOs directly without Model.

questions.question: In Thymeleaf view, how is user accessed if added as below?
questions.explanation: model.addAttribute("user", user) exposes user in template as ${user} (name matches attribute key).

questions.question: What does @Valid on a controller parameter do?
options: Triggers Bean Validation (JSR-380) on the object
questions.explanation: @Valid runs constraints like @NotNull, @Size on the argument. Combine with BindingResult to handle errors without exception.

questions.question: Which annotation rejects null and blank strings on a field?
questions.explanation: @NotBlank (javax/jakarta.validation) ensures trimmed length > 0. @NotNull allows empty string; @NotEmpty for collections.

questions.question: How should validation errors be handled when using @Valid without exception?
questions.explanation: Signature: method(@Valid @RequestBody Dto dto, BindingResult br). If br.hasErrors(), return 400 with field errors.

questions.question: What does @ExceptionHandler on a controller method do?
options: Maps specific exceptions to response for that controller
questions.explanation: @ExceptionHandler(IllegalArgumentException.class) in @ControllerAdvice or controller returns structured error response.

questions.question: Why use @ControllerAdvice?
options: Global exception handling and model binding across controllers
questions.explanation: @ControllerAdvice centralizes @ExceptionHandler, @InitBinder, @ModelAttribute methods for all or selected controllers.

questions.question: What HTTP status fits ResourceNotFoundException handler returning NOT_FOUND?
questions.explanation: @ResponseStatus(HttpStatus.NOT_FOUND) or ResponseEntity.status(404) signals client the resource id does not exist.

questions.question: Why use DTOs instead of exposing JPA entities in REST APIs?
options: Decouple persistence model, control serialized fields, avoid lazy-load and circular JSON
questions.explanation: Entities carry persistence annotations and associations; DTOs expose stable API contracts and prevent leaking internal structure.

questions.question: What is a common way to map Entity to DTO?
questions.explanation: Teams use explicit mappers (MapStruct compile-time), libraries, or hand-written conversion in service layer.

questions.question: What does Spring Data Pageable represent?
options: Request for a page: page number, size, and optional Sort
questions.explanation: Pageable encapsulates page, size, sort. Repository methods return Page<T> with content and total elements.

questions.question: Which repository method signature supports pagination?
questions.explanation: Spring Data derives queries; Pageable adds LIMIT/OFFSET (or cursor) and count query for totals.

questions.question: What metadata does Page<T> typically include?
questions.explanation: Page interface provides getContent(), getTotalElements(), getTotalPages(), isFirst(), isLast() for API pagination wrappers.

questions.question: What is OpenAPI (Swagger) used for in Spring projects?
options: Describing REST API contract; generating UI docs and clients
questions.explanation: OpenAPI documents paths, schemas, security. springdoc-openapi exposes /swagger-ui and /v3/api-docs.

questions.question: Which springdoc annotation documents operation summary on a controller method?
questions.explanation: @Operation(summary = "...") from io.swagger.v3.oas.annotations documents individual endpoints in OpenAPI.

questions.question: What does @Schema on a DTO field control?
questions.explanation: @Schema(description, example, requiredMode) enriches generated OpenAPI for clients and Swagger UI.

questions.question: What does @RequestBody do?
options: Deserializes HTTP body (JSON) to Java object via HttpMessageConverter
questions.explanation: @RequestBody uses Jackson (for JSON) to map body to parameter type. Often paired with @Valid.

questions.question: What does @ResponseBody do?
options: Serializes return value directly to HTTP response body
questions.explanation: @ResponseBody selects HttpMessageConverter to write JSON/XML. @RestController applies it class-wide.

questions.question: What is DispatcherServlet's role?
options: Front controller delegating to handler mappings, adapters, and view resolvers
questions.explanation: DispatcherServlet is the single entry servlet mapping requests to @RequestMapping handlers and handling response rendering.

questions.question: What does @Email validate?
options: String matches email format per Bean Validation
questions.explanation: @Email checks basic email pattern (configurable). Uniqueness still needs service/repository check.

questions.question: What is Method Validation (@Validated on class)?
options: Validates method parameters and return values on service layer with @Valid constraints
questions.explanation: @Validated on @Service enables @NotNull parameters on methods via AOP MethodValidationPostProcessor.

questions.question: When is @ResponseStatus insufficient compared to ResponseEntity?
options: When you need custom headers, body shape, or dynamic status per call
questions.explanation: ResponseEntity lets you build status, headers, and body programmatically; @ResponseStatus is declarative fixed status.

questions.question: Why avoid returning List<Entity> with bidirectional @OneToMany in JSON?
options: Infinite recursion / huge graphs (Jackson JsonIgnore or DTOs needed)
questions.explanation: Parent→children→parent causes StackOverflow or huge payloads. Use DTOs or @JsonIgnore on back-reference.

questions.question: Client sends GET /api/users?page=1&size=20&sort=name,asc. What binds sort?
options: Pageable or PageableDefault with Sort parameter
questions.explanation: Spring MVC resolves Pageable from query params page, size, sort automatically when argument is present.

questions.question: Where is springdoc OpenAPI JSON typically exposed?
questions.explanation: springdoc-openapi serves OpenAPI 3 JSON at /v3/api-docs and Swagger UI at /swagger-ui.html or /swagger-ui/index.html.

questions.question: What does @ModelAttribute on a method parameter do?
options: Binds request parameters/form fields to object
questions.explanation: @ModelAttribute binds query/form data to command object—common in traditional MVC forms, less in pure REST JSON.

questions.question: Content negotiation in REST means?
options: Selecting response format (JSON/XML) via Accept header or produces attribute
questions.explanation: @GetMapping(produces = MediaType.APPLICATION_JSON_VALUE) limits mapping to clients accepting that media type.

questions.question: What does ProblemDetail (RFC 7807) provide in Spring 6+?
options: Standardized machine-readable error body (type, title, status, detail)
questions.explanation: ProblemDetail helps consistent API errors instead of ad-hoc JSON shapes across services.

questions.question: What does @Size(min=8) on password enforce?
options: String length or collection size constraints
questions.explanation: @Size validates character length for strings or element count for collections per min/max attributes.

questions.question: Record types as DTOs in Java 16+ offer?
options: Compact immutable data carriers good for API responses
questions.explanation: Records provide equals/hashCode/toString and accessors—nice for read-only API DTOs with validation on compact constructors.

questions.question: What status should POST /orders return on successful creation with body?
questions.explanation: REST convention: 201 Created for new resource; Location header may point to GET /orders/{id}.

questions.question: What does @Tag on a controller class do?
questions.explanation: @Tag(name = "Orders") organizes operations in Swagger UI sidebar sections.

questions.question: Offset pagination drawback on large tables?
options: Deep pages (high OFFSET) scan many rows and slow down
questions.explanation: OFFSET 100000 LIMIT 20 still scans skipped rows. Cursor/keyset pagination scales better for infinite feeds.

questions.question: Should generic Exception handler expose stack traces to clients?
options: No—log server-side; return safe message to avoid information leakage
questions.explanation: Production APIs return generic errors; detailed traces belong in logs and monitoring, not public responses.

questions.question: What does consumes attribute on @PostMapping specify?
questions.explanation: consumes = APPLICATION_JSON_VALUE maps only when client sends matching Content-Type header.

questions.question: Custom constraint in Bean Validation requires?
questions.explanation: Define annotation annotated with @Constraint(validatedBy=...) and implement ConstraintValidator<A, T> for logic.
